## [12:14 - 15:14]

There's a thing that's really important to understand about family businesses is that there's two different codes going on in terms of communication and hierarchy. And we call this the secondary power structure. So in a business, you have, for instance, a president or a CEO or, you know, high executive and then you have management and then you have people working under management. That's just an example. And so the person, the CEO, the president, whoever, is the most powerful and they hire the managers and they fire the managers and the managers hire and fire the people below them. Right? So that would be the typical thing. In a family business, there's a completely different power structure. So you have, for instance, hiring and firing is often not feasible. There are often networks of affections and loyalties that don't obey that simple hierarchy. Right? So like if the president, if the family business, the president's wife or husband has a strong affection for someone who, and raised someone who's in the company. And sometimes that person lower-looking, you know, we call it a cross-generation coalition, meaning has power over the CEO. Right? You can complain on TV or whatever and on TV, we'll get on the CEO's back and say, it'd be nice to bust her. Right? So what happens, the confusion that happens here is that in an ordinary company, if you have someone who's underneath you and they show disrespect and you feel like they don't respect your leadership or they don't, they won't listen to you or they question you or they do insubordinate stuff, that's often just like a red slide, you've got to get rid of them. It's not worth trying to fight that kind of bad attitude. Correct. But in a family, and you have generational differences, then you have a totally different code. Often, the younger generation will grieve about the older generation and say that I had a date. And you know what I mean? That's kind of normal. They give you a fight and make up and stuff like that. So what has to happen here is Monty has to get that he's triggering the code of getting out of fire or someone. Right. You know what I mean? When you question the company leaders, you're triggering the, got to get rid of this person, you know, it's time to fire them, kind of code. And I don't know if that will help. You know, I think the fact that the uncle of 79 is probably just as relevant, but he needs to get it. That has a business owner, that there's a certain propriety and structure that's done.
